response: solution The erroneous code likely fails by not properly handling duplicate elements when constructing the wiggle pattern, especially when the most frequent element appears too often. solution To fix this, we should first sort the array and then place elements from the second half into even indices and the first half into odd indices, which helps separate duplicates. solution However, for an O(n) time complexity solution without full sorting, we can use the median-finding algorithm (QuickSelect) to partition the array around the median. solution After finding the median, we perform a virtual indexing strategy where we map the original indices to a new order that interleaves elements from the left and right partitions. solution This approach ensures that equal elements are spaced apart as much as possible, satisfying the condition that adjacent elements are not equal if a valid arrangement exists. solution For negative integers, the logic remains identical since the comparison operators work correctly regardless of the sign of the numbers. solution The final implementation uses QuickSelect to find the median in O(n) average time and then rearranges the array in O(n) time using the virtual index mapping.
